Career leaderboard, World Championships 2013–2025

By default, Declarers are ranked by their comparative IMP result (averaged) vs the field on hands where they played the same contract from the same seat.

min boards 200 boards  · 
About this data

Built from results scraped from the last seven World Team Championships (2013–2025). Open, Women, Seniors and Mixed are ranked separately.

comp bd = boards in this comparison / total boards declared. Every column is averaged over the left number: the boards that had another table in the same contract at the matched seat (so the whole row describes one consistent board set). It moves with the seat/side toggle; boards where no one else played the contract have no field and are excluded. Double-dummy is still evaluated for the actual declaring direction. · tricks v DD tricks vs double-dummy (declarer) / holding declarer below DD (defence) · tricks v field tricks vs the same-contract field · make v field making (declarer) / defeating (defence) vs the field · IMP v field that trick edge scored in IMPs, the ranking. Green beats the field, red trails it. Champs = number of championships · span. Click a column heading to sort; click any country to highlight it. Seat/side toggle = whether peers must be the exact declarer seat (default) or either seat of the side.

The career IMP figure regresses toward zero vs single-event figures (more boards, more averaging), so the spread is tighter; a sustained +0.5 IMP/board over 400+ boards across multiple world championships is elite. Declarer, defender-individual and partnership are three separate rankings; defenders are pooled across all partners, partnerships only over boards those two played together.
